title: Tourmaline and Clean Energy Open New CNG Stations, Advancing Western Canada's First Commercial-Grade Natural Gas Fueling Corridor
content: Tourmaline Oil Corp. and Clean Energy Fuels Corp. have opened two new compressed natural gas (CNG) fueling stations in Calgary and Grande Prairie, Alberta, creating a transportation corridor with an existing station in Edmonton. This marks a significant step in their joint $70 million plan to build Western Canada's first public CNG network, aimed at reducing diesel use in trucking. The network will eventually include up to 20 stations, potentially fueling 3,000 trucks daily. Mullen Group, a key supporter, plans to expand its CNG fleet, and Alberta Premier Danielle Smith praised the initiative for promoting sustainable transportation solutions.
author: Analysts Account
